extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) in 404 error handler. The request.url is directly interpolated into a template string using Python % formatting on line 112 (''' % request.url). This untrusted user input is then rendered with render_template_string() on line 114, which evaluates Jinja2 templates. Additionally, dangerous builtins (dir, help, locals) are passed as template context, enabling Remote Code Execution (RCE) if an attacker crafts a malicious URL.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Templating Engine
CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: SQL Injection vulnerability in /search endpoint. User-supplied 'search' parameter from JSON request body is directly interpolated into a raw SQL query string using Python % formatting on line 261: str_query = "SELECT first_name, last_name, username FROM customer WHERE username = '%s';" % search_term. The unsanitized query is then executed directly via db.engine.execute(str_query) on line 265. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL syntax to bypass authentication, exfiltrate data, or modify database records.

Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) in error handler of /search endpoint. When the SQL query raises an exception, the exception message str(e) is interpolated into a template string via % formatting on line 280. This exception message is then rendered with render_template_string() on line 281 with dangerous builtins (dir, help, locals) in the context. An attacker can trigger an SQL error and inject Jinja2 template syntax in the search parameter to achieve Remote Code Execution (RCE).

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Templating Engine
CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: Insecure YAML deserialization via yaml.load() without a safe Loader on user-uploaded file. PyYAML's yaml.load() function (line 329) without an explicit Loader argument defaults to the full Loader which can instantiate arbitrary Python objects. An attacker can upload a maliciously crafted YAML file containing !!python/object/apply:os.system directives or other object instantiation payloads to achieve Remote Code Execution (RCE).

XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability via user-uploaded DOCX file parsing. User-uploaded .docx files are parsed using python-docx's Document() constructor on line 303 without XXE protections. DOCX files are ZIP archives containing XML. With versions like python-docx 0.8.5 and lxml 4.2.1, maliciously crafted OOXML documents with external entity references can be processed. An attacker can craft a DOCX file with embedded XXE payloads to read local files, perform SSRF, or achieve Remote Code Execution.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
